tokens: We'd like to try Auto Track again on the steerable.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: And how's our friendly Command Module Pilot doing?
Is he going to be ready to pick us up with a nominal launch time?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: You bet, Al, I've been talking with him all morning here, and he's really whipping around, getting a lot of pictures, and doing a lot of
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Just visual on this last pass, though.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Did he ever get that going?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Ron, the ALSEP, from the top of Cone Crater, is so bright, it stands out like a little jewel.
I'm not surprised at all that Stu could see it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: He was really convinced that that's what he saw.
And he didn't even know where it was, you know, and asked me where it was, and I came back, and sure enough confirmed it, and that's where it was.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Tell them that this high gain antenna is setting here, kind of wobbling, and making all sorts of racket, when it should be setting very still and quiet, and was until just a minute or so ago.
Now it seems to be starting to go unstable, or at least neutrally stable, and it's not driving wildly, but it's making a hell of a racket.
It's just kind of wobbling, around a neutral point.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, INCO copies that, and we'll let you know on it.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay, they've made some configuration change on the ground station hookup down here to you; and for some reason, they think that that may have helped your antenna chatter, or whatever.
The thing seem to be steadier now?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: It steadied out for a minute or so, and now it's picking up again.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Why don't they accept the fact that the damn thing is about to quit on us?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: They'd like you to go back and select the lunar stay - the erectable.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Houston, Antares; we have a crew's status report for you.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Your old Maroon Team on station down here.
We'd like to go to the pre-lift-off comm configuration, as modified earlier, to check out the High Bit Rate Telemetry lock.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, Bruce, are you ready to try that?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Antares, I'm reading you loud with a good bit of background noise on the circuit, as you might expect; but comm's okay.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Thank you, Bruce, and I'll return to the other setup.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We would like for you to stay in this configuration for the time being so we can see how comm and telemetry holds in.
(Very loud static lasting about a minute)
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: We'd like you to return to the previous comm configuration, utilizing the erectable antenna.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, Ed; whenever you are through eating, would you give us a call, please?
And I've got a batch of Pads to pass up to you.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I'll be ready to copy here in about 30 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Bruce, what do you want to send me first?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll send up the ascent Pad for the direct rendezvous first.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Ascent Pad for coelliptic-type rendezvous follows when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: The balance of the Pad is N/A (not applicable).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And that next to the last one should be 5...Yeah, plus 56968.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And now I have the CSI Pad, itself, for you.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (Pause) Hey, can you give us P00 and Data?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Standing by for a CSI Pad readback.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Noun 11 is 143:26:36.60; 145:11:30.00; Noun 81, plus 051.6, plus all zeros; and 0206.6, 0311.5, plus 051.6, plus all zeros, and is it plus or minus 001.1?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The last value is positive; that is, plus 001.1.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And we're starting the uplink for you.
I now have the consumables update.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Consumables update for 140 hours even (139:20 of transcript time): RCS Alfa, 80; Bravo, 78; descent oxygen, 38 percent; ascent tank 2, 97 percent; tank 1 reading is invalid, but it's approximately the same quantity.
Descent water, 23 percent; ascent water, 98 percent each; descent ampere-hours, 488; ascent, 572.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: At 140 hours, RCS is 80, 78; Descent O2 is 38, Ascent is 97 percent, and probably 97 percent; water is 23; ascent is 98, 98; ampere hours: descent is 488, ascent is 572.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) Then I've got an update to your Time Line Book and an update to the Surface Checklist.
Let's hold off on the Surface Checklist update, but the Time Line Book whenever you're ready.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: On page 14 of the Time Line Book.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Down immediately prior to the block that says 60 contact.
We want you to insert a Verb 48 DAP (Digital Autopilot) load; and in R1 of the DAP, we're looking for 13002 to give you four-jet translation in the docking procedure.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And immediately after the 60 contact block, we would like to add in "TTCA Commander"...Let me read through it once quickly, and then I'll go through it again slowly, if you want to write it down verbatim.
TTCA Commander: thrust plus-X at contact, until CMP confirms capture or for 10 seconds whichever occurs first.

And then, under "Confirm docking with CSM," change that to "Confirm capture report to CSM (means 'from CSM')." Would you like me to go through it a word or two at a time so you can write it out, or do you just want to make a notation to that effect?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: No, you want to confirm capture as plus-X until
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: It is my understanding that the docking originally will be tried in the nominal fashion.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: This is a modification to the nominal procedure.
We anticipate using it on the first attempt.
If, of course, Stu calls "Capture," prior to the time that you start thrusting, why that would not be necessary.
But, nominally, you would start your plus-X thrusting when you feel a good solid contact.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: This is even on the first attempt at docking?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: In your Lunar Surface Book, we're deleting the uplink at lift-off minus 35 minutes.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, also, in the Surface Checklist on page 8-1, under your S-band comm configuration that reads "S-Band-PM, Primary, Primary, Voice, PCM, Off/Reset", we're changing that to "PM, Primary, Down Voice Backup", in accordance with the comm configuration we've passed to you previously.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: We're going to lift-off in Down Voice Backup, then.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That's affirmative, unless we instruct you otherwise later on.
And you can delete all references to the steerable antenna, such as that found on page 8-6, putting you in Track Mode, Auto, and all that.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I thought that we were going to go off (that is, launch in) Normal; and, if we had trouble, go to this new procedure.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Our baseline, now, is to lift-off in this mode.
We expect the Omni comm to improve as you fly through the profile and pitchover.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, in this connection, since you are lifting off in ICS/PTT, we won't be monitoring the intercom loop within the cabin, and we'd like to encourage you to comment freely on how things are progressing and read stuff out to us, as the occasion seems appropriate.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Well, we never comment freely, but we'll comment reasonably.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We're starting through our checklist, at lift-off minus one (hour and) 15 (minutes on Surface checklist page 8-1).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Having passed you the changes through the comm configuration at minus 1 hour and 15 minutes, we'd like to hold off on going into the Down-Voice Backup mode and ICS/PTT until lift-off minus five-zero minutes.
That is, just prior to the RCS hot-fire check, as we're advised that on Apollo 12, the hot-fire check blew the erectable antenna over.
If the erectable antenna is still standing after the hot-fire check, we'd prefer to come back into the Normal voice configuration until sometime shortly before lift-off.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Why don't you call the comm in real time and we'll respond?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Latest revision to the communications plan.

At this time, we would like you to select the steerable antenna and Auto Track Mode, and give us your evaluation of the amount of grinding and motor noise you get out of it and try to form an opinion whether we think it will be satisfactory for lift-off.

If it seems like it's making too much noise or behaving erratically at the present time, we will then go into the Slew position on the steerable antenna, maintain comm up until just prior to lift-off, where we want to return to Auto and attempt to make it in the Auto position.
If it proves unsatisfactory during ascent, we'll request Aft Omni, Down Voice Backup and ICS/PTT.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: It's nice and quiet for the moment.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'll let you know if it starts...
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: ...during ascent, if you perceive that you've lost the steerable, why don't wait for us to call you to switch over?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Some of them are on the ascent Pad, Bruce, but there's some that aren't.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Except for those values which are loaded on the Pad, the ones you have in the checklist are good.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: The rendezvous radar test is satisfactory.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Houston, are you ready for the hot-fire of the jets (on
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, Houston, the hot-fire check's complete.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: It looked good from down here.
I have your K-factor update (as per Sur 8-6).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Antares, Houston; K factor: 140, plus 00, plus 00.36.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, with respect to the comm situation again, Ed, if you have to switch to the Aft Omni antenna prior to making the 30-degree yaw maneuver, delete the yaw maneuver; that is, remain in the belly band.
If you have to switch after making the yaw maneuver, do not change; that is, remain with the 30-degree yaw.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, for your information, due to the CSM orbit, which is currently about 61.5 by 58.2, we're anticipating a TPI burn Delta-V on the order of 100 feet per second, vice (means "versus") the lower 60 feet ...
(correcting himself) vice the 90 feet per second in the flight plan.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, for your information, Antares, your thruster firings were also monitored by the seismometer.
You're coming through loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: We'd like to get Batts 5 and 6 on line now, and 1 and 3, Off, if you can do it without interrupting the P57 (which is the platform alignment program).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (Long Pause) Have it (in) just a second, Bruce.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We copy your address 47 and 53 (near the bottom, right-hand column of Sur 8-9).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Do we want to stay with the computed 047 and 053, or shall I reload the Pad?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That's affirmative, Ed; and we're only reading four digits on the DEDA (Digital Entry and Display Assembly).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: To reload or to stay with what I have?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Barely able to stop himself from laughing) The "affirmative" was to stay with the computed values that are already in the computer.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You can take descent battery 2 off, as per the checklist.
Keep descent battery 4 on-line until our call.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, Houston; we're standing by to pressurize the ascent (propellant tanks with) helium.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: You're Go on that, one at a time, please.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You are Go on the second (meaning Helium Tank 2).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You are Go for lift-off this pass; direct rendezvous; guidance control, PGNS (Primary Guidance and Navigation System).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You can treat Batts 2 and 4 per the checklist, over.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Both batteries 5 and 6 are looking good.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: A mark at 4 minutes; stand by...
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Well, I can read you somewhat.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And, Antares, Kitty Hawk is trying to raise you on VHF.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (Very faint) Read you about 3 by, Al.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Kitty Hawk is reading you 3 by on VHF.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And Antares is counting down to 2 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Little less than 2 minutes; everything is Go.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: The A and B lights are on.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Antares has got ascent engine armed.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You're coming through loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, be up to see you shortly.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (To Roosa) Have a nice cool one (that is, a cold beer) set up.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And here we're going across Cat's Paw.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You're looking good from down here, Al.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (Pause) Little bit low and slow, but PGNS are...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, you want to give me a 623?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Take a look at the target again.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And you're Go from down here.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: The steering is still good, Houston; coming up on 3 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: V-sub-I is good, H-dot is good, H is good; PGNS and AGS agree.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, we're getting an oscillation in our RCS pressures, but I'm sure it's (garbled, probably "instrumentation").
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: RCS looks good from down here, Ed.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And you're Go from the ground at 3 and one-half.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Antares is still Go from the ground.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: H-dot is good; H is good; (PGNS and)
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You're Go from the ground; looking good.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: We show all sources - PGNS, AGS, and MSFN - in good agreement.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: About 2:25 (two minutes twenty-five seconds) to go; out-of-plane looks good.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: PGNS, AGS, and MSFN are all in good agreement.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (To Ed) Now, you can stop your (sequence) camera, if you want.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We'll get it (the Noun 85 E, 500R checks of the AGS and PGNS) at 5:30.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Garbled, undoubtedly "V-sub-I"); H-dot's good; H is good; PGNS and AGS agree.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (LetÕs) take a look at (PGNS Noun) 85 (on the DSKY showing the velocities to be gained on each LM axis) versus (AGS DEDA address) 500 (velocity yet to be gained along the thrust axis) for a minute.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Looking at Noun 85 on the DSKY) Okay, 946 (feet-per-sec to be gained along the thrust axis).
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Main SOV's (Shut-Off Valves) Open; Ascent Feeds, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We've had a shutdown, on the PGNS.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Roger; trim the PGNS, all axes.
transcript.speaker: McCandless
